input.headline: Wales to host Bosnia in World Cup qualifying play-off semi-final

raw.headline: Wales to host Bosnia in World Cup qualifying play-off semi-final

winner: Wales will be hosting Bosnia in a World Cup qualifying play-off semi-final—just don't ask which half they're playing on.

candidates.text: Why did Bosnia bring a ladder to the Wales match? Because they heard the game was a *semi-final* climb!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'semi-final' and 'climb' are clever and specific to the context, with a concise and surprising twist."}

candidates.text: Why did the football match between Wales and Bosnia get moved to a library? Because they needed a quiet place to discuss the *qualifying* for the World Cup — and nobody wanted to hear about the semi-final *play-off* again.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the library twist, but the pun on 'qualifying' and 'play-off' feels a bit forced and lacks clarity. It's not fully surprising or specific enough to be highly funny."}

candidates.text: Wales is prepared to host Bosnia, but they are still confused about whether they should provide a stadium or just a very large plate of pâté.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist on hosting a match with pâté, concise and funny."}

candidates.text: Wales are thrilled to host Bosnia, while the Bosnian fans are just relieved they don't have to host the traffic.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific comparison, with a clever twist on hosting responsibilities."}

candidates.text: Wales is ready to face Bosnia, but they’re worried the match might be a bit of a Bos-ny.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'Bosnia' with 'Bos-ny' are clever and specific, leveraging the football context for surprise and humor."}

candidates.text: Wales will be hosting Bosnia in a World Cup qualifying play-off semi-final—just don't ask which half they're playing on.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific, playing on the ambiguity of 'half' (as in football halves or geographical halves). It's slightly forced but still humorous."}

candidates.text: Wales tried out for a football team, but the coach said, "You're too good—you should probably qualify for Bosnia.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the twist of Wales qualifying for Bosnia, but it's a bit forced and lacks clarity. The reference to 'trying out for a football team' doesn't clearly tie into the headline, reducing surprise and specificity."}

candidates.text: Wales is set to face Bosnia in a World Cup qualifier—looks like the real battle will be between the sheep and the footballers.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the reference to sheep is a bit generic and may not resonate strongly with all audiences."}

candidates.text: Wales has invited Bosnia to a friendly game, but forgot to mention it's actually a semi-final in the World Cup qualifying play-off.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the setup is a bit generic and relies on a common misunderstanding rather than a clever twist."}
